metadata.name: UNIQA Conversion Coach Rule Configuration
metadata.purpose: Maps sanitized browser behavior signals from the Chrome extension to transparent coach interventions.
metadata.importantNote: This file is not mock coach data. It is the configurable rule base used by the local Coach API to decide which real intervention to return.

scope.outOfScopeHandling: Out-of-scope choices are routed to advice. They are valid exits, but not counted as online conversions.

runtimeNotes: Rules are evaluated from top to bottom. The first matching rule wins.
Each rule returns the first intervention in its intervention list that has not already been shown in the same session.
Routing interventions use bypassBudget because they are scope guards, not persuasion nudges.
The API ignores descriptive metadata fields, but they are kept here so teammates can review the logic without reading TypeScript.

long_dwell.meaning: User spent at least the dwell threshold on a step.
long_dwell.example: The visitor stays on the tariff comparison table without continuing.
long_dwell.riskInterpretation: Possible confusion, comparison fatigue, or hesitation.

back_nav.meaning: User clicked a back or cancel-like control.
back_nav.example: The visitor goes back from the price page.

repeated_change.meaning: User changed the same input multiple times.
repeated_change.example: The visitor repeatedly adjusts social insurance or form fields.

cancel_intent.meaning: User hovered cancel/back controls or became inactive.
cancel_intent.example: The visitor pauses for a long time or hovers near a close/back target.
cancel_intent.riskInterpretation: Possible intent to leave the flow.

price_fixation.meaning: Price hover appears together with dwell or recent price attention.
price_fixation.example: The visitor keeps hovering the tariff price area.
price_fixation.riskInterpretation: Possible price shock or need for value framing.

oos_tariff.example: The visitor clicks Opt. Plus or Premium.
oos_tariff.riskInterpretation: The selected product cannot complete online in this prototype scope.

oos_path.meaning: User selected a non-online scope path.
oos_path.example: The visitor selects hospital coverage or insurance for other persons.
oos_path.riskInterpretation: The journey should route to advice instead of pretending conversion is possible.

price_gap_shock.meaning: Current or final price is at least priceGapShockThresholdEur above the initial visible price.
price_gap_shock.example: The final calculated premium is higher than the first premium shown.
price_gap_shock.riskInterpretation: Possible late-stage abandonment from a perceived unexpected increase.

none.riskInterpretation: No coach intervention should be shown.

s1_coverage_scope.name: Coverage scope
s1_coverage_scope.goal: Confirm the visitor wants private-doctor coverage rather than an out-of-scope hospital path.
s1_coverage_scope.mainRisk: The visitor selects hospital coverage, which requires advice.

s2_for_whom.name: Insured person
s2_for_whom.goal: Confirm the visitor is insuring themself only.
s2_for_whom.mainRisk: The visitor selects other persons, which requires advice.

s3_personal_data.name: Quote basics
s3_personal_data.goal: Collect enough information to calculate a preliminary premium.
s3_personal_data.mainRisk: Sensitive-data hesitation before the value of the product is visible.

s4_initial_price.name: Initial price and tariff choice
s4_initial_price.goal: Help the visitor choose Start or Optimal and continue online.
s4_initial_price.mainRisk: Price shock, comparison shopping, or clicks on advisor-only tariffs.

s5_add_ons.name: Optional add-ons
s5_add_ons.goal: Clarify that add-ons are optional and keep the visitor moving.

s6_health_questions.name: Health questions
s6_health_questions.goal: Collect required health information for final assessment.
s6_health_questions.mainRisk: Form fatigue or trust concerns around medical data.

s7_final_price.name: Final price
s7_final_price.goal: Explain any final premium change and keep qualified users in the online flow.
s7_final_price.mainRisk: Late-stage abandonment caused by a higher final price.

policy.description: Global guardrails for how often the coach can intervene and which events count as hesitation.
policy.maxInterventionsReason: Keeps the assistant helpful without becoming noisy. Scope-routing messages do not count against this budget.
policy.hesitationEventsReason: These are behavioral signs that the user may need explanation, reassurance, or value framing.
policy.priceGapShockThresholdReason: A final-price increase of EUR 3 or more is treated as meaningful enough to explain.

actionDefaults.description: Default UI behavior for coach cards unless an intervention overrides it.

placementByStep.description: Step-specific default placements. Price and add-on decisions are placed near the primary CTA because the coach should support the next decision.

none.label: No action
none.rationale: Used when no rule matches or budget is exhausted.

simplified_explanation.label: Simplified explanation
simplified_explanation.intent: Reduce cognitive load when the visitor hesitates on optional choices.
simplified_explanation.title: Tarife einfach einordnen
simplified_explanation.body: Start und Optimal sind die online abschliessbaren Tarife. Optimal deckt mehr Leistungen ab, ohne in den Beratungspfad zu wechseln.

cta.label: Weiter
cta.prompt: Help me understand whether I should continue with or without optional add-ons.

shownWhen: A later policy can reuse this for general comparison confusion
simplified_explanation.rationale: Keeps the user in the online path by explaining the decision without hiding complexity.

trust_signal.label: Trust signal
trust_signal.intent: Explain why sensitive information is requested before the user abandons.
trust_signal.title: Warum diese Daten noetig sind
trust_signal.body: Geburtsdatum und Sozialversicherung werden fuer die vorlaeufige Praemienberechnung genutzt. Es wird noch nichts abgeschlossen.

cta.label: Zum naechsten Feld
cta.prompt: Explain why this information is needed and what happens next.

shownWhen: User hesitates on personal quote data
User shows cancel intent near final confirmation
trust_signal.rationale: The coach addresses trust friction without collecting or storing raw sensitive values.

price_reframe.label: Price reframe
price_reframe.intent: Make the monthly premium easier to understand by reframing it as daily value.
price_reframe.title: Preis kurz eingeordnet
price_reframe.body: Optimal liegt hier bei rund EUR 2,25 pro Tag und bleibt vollstaendig online abschliessbar.

cta.label: Tarife vergleichen
cta.prompt: Help me compare the online tariffs and understand the current monthly price.

shownWhen: User appears close to leaving on initial price
price_reframe.rationale: The price page is the strongest known drop-off point, so the coach explains value without discounting or changing the product.

market_comparison.label: Market comparison
market_comparison.intent: Help comparison-oriented users understand whether the premium is reasonable.
market_comparison.title: Marktvergleich
market_comparison.body: Mit rund EUR 68 pro Monat liegt Optimal im unteren Drittel vergleichbarer Privatarzt-Tarife in Oesterreich.

cta.label: Optimal pruefen
cta.prompt: Help me compare Optimal with the other online-completable tariff.

shownWhen: User navigates backward from the price page
Price reframe has already been shown in this journey
market_comparison.rationale: Prevents comparison-shopping abandonment by providing an in-flow benchmark.

price_gap_transparency.label: Price gap transparency
price_gap_transparency.intent: Explain why final price may differ from the first visible estimate.
price_gap_transparency.title: Finaler Preis erklaert
price_gap_transparency.body: Der finale Preis beruecksichtigt Ihr persoenliches Gesundheitsprofil. Sie koennen den Abschluss weiterhin online fortsetzen.

cta.label: Weiter pruefen
cta.prompt: Explain why the final price changed and whether I can still continue online.

shownWhen: Final price increase crosses the configured threshold
User shows cancel intent at final price
price_gap_transparency.rationale: Late-stage price changes create abandonment risk unless the reason is made explicit.

tariff_route_explainer.label: Advisor-only tariff explainer
tariff_route_explainer.intent: Recover users who click advisor-only tariffs by pointing them back to the online-completable options.
tariff_route_explainer.title: Opt. Plus und Premium brauchen Beratung
tariff_route_explainer.body: Opt. Plus und Premium brauchen Beratung. Fuer einen direkten Online-Abschluss bleiben Start und Optimal die passenden Optionen.

cta.label: Optimal pruefen
cta.prompt: Help me understand why Optimal can be completed online and how it compares.

tariff_route_explainer.rationale: The user expressed tariff interest, so this keeps the online path honest while preserving a route to completion.

advisor_route.label: Advisor route
advisor_route.intent: Cleanly route journeys that cannot be completed online in this prototype scope.
advisor_route.title: Dieser Pfad braucht Beratung
advisor_route.body: Krankenhaus oder weitere versicherte Personen brauchen persoenliche Beratung. Dieser Pfad ist ein sauberer Beratungsausstieg.

cta.label: Beratung anzeigen
cta.prompt: Explain why this journey should move to personal advice and what I can do next.

advisor_route.rationale: Out-of-scope paths should not be treated as failed online conversions. They should become valid advisor exits.

save_progress.label: Save progress
save_progress.intent: Offer a lower-pressure continuation path when final-stage hesitation persists.
save_progress.title: Fortschritt nicht verlieren
save_progress.body: Speichern Sie Ihren Fortschritt und setzen Sie den Abschluss spaeter ohne erneute Eingabe fort.

cta.label: Fortschritt sichern
cta.prompt: Help me save or understand my progress before I leave.

shownWhen: Final price transparency and trust signal were already shown
save_progress.rationale: Keeps a qualified user from losing progress without forcing immediate purchase.

rules.name: Route out-of-scope path selections to advice
rules.userSituation: The visitor selected hospital coverage or another insured person.
rules.businessReason: This path cannot be completed in the online Start/Optimal prototype scope.
rules.expectedOutcome: User understands that advice is required instead of seeing a misleading online nudge.
rules.implementationNote: This rule has highest priority because scope correctness matters more than conversion nudging.

rules.name: Explain advisor-only tariffs and point back to online options
rules.userSituation: The visitor clicked Opt. Plus or Premium on the initial price page.
rules.businessReason: Opt. Plus and Premium require advice, but Start and Optimal can still convert online.
rules.expectedOutcome: User can continue with Optimal instead of abandoning or entering an unsupported path.
rules.implementationNote: Placed before generic price rules so tariff scope is explained first.

rules.name: Reassure user during quote data entry
rules.userSituation: The visitor hesitates while entering date of birth or social insurance information.
rules.businessReason: Sensitive data questions can create trust friction before the quote is even visible.
rules.expectedOutcome: User understands why the data is needed and continues to the tariff page.
rules.implementationNote: Uses the global hesitation group to cover dwell, repeated changes, back navigation, cancel intent, and price fixation.

rules.name: Reframe initial price when user fixates or may abandon
rules.userSituation: The visitor spends attention on the premium or appears close to leaving.
rules.businessReason: The initial price step is a critical drop-off point.
rules.expectedOutcome: User gets a concise value frame and is encouraged to compare tariffs instead of leaving.
rules.implementationNote: If price_reframe was already shown, the API falls back to market_comparison.

rules.name: Benchmark price when user navigates backward
rules.userSituation: The visitor backs away from the tariff page.
rules.businessReason: Back navigation at price often means comparison shopping or unresolved price concern.
rules.expectedOutcome: User receives a market benchmark before abandoning the quote.
rules.implementationNote: Runs after price fixation because explicit price fixation gets the stronger daily-value frame first.

rules.name: Clarify optional add-ons
rules.userSituation: The visitor hesitates on optional add-ons.
rules.businessReason: Add-ons can create decision fatigue and slow down online completion.
rules.expectedOutcome: User understands extras are optional and can continue without feeling blocked.
rules.implementationNote: The rule explains the choice. It does not remove add-ons or auto-select anything.

rules.name: Reassure user during medical data entry
rules.userSituation: The visitor hesitates while answering medical and doctor questions.
rules.businessReason: Medical data questions create privacy friction late enough that a user is otherwise qualified.
rules.expectedOutcome: User understands why the data is needed and continues to final price calculation.
rules.implementationNote: Uses the earlier s6 inactivity timer plus repeated-change/back/cancel signals.

rules.name: Explain final price increase
rules.userSituation: The visitor sees a final premium increase or shows abandonment intent near final price.
rules.businessReason: Late-stage surprise is one of the most damaging abandonment moments.
rules.expectedOutcome: User understands why the price changed and can still continue online.
rules.implementationNote: The ordered fallback sequence moves from transparency to reassurance to save-progress retention.

rules.name: Reassure at final confirmation
rules.userSituation: The visitor appears close to leaving on the final confirmation step.
rules.businessReason: Final hesitation may come from trust or commitment anxiety.
rules.expectedOutcome: User receives a calm reassurance before deciding whether to complete.
rules.implementationNote: This step is disabled in the current page map until verified, but the rule is ready for the full funnel.
